output.content:
1|---
     2|name: platforms
     3|description: Use this skill whenever the user asks to create, scaffold, add, or
     4|  set up an app in the current Rork workspace, including requests like "create
     5|  an iOS app for this website" or adding a native iOS/Swift, Android/Kotlin,
     6|  Expo/React Native, or web app alongside an existing app. This unlocks the
     7|  createApp tool; do not reject app-creation requests just because another app
     8|  already exists.
     9|---
    10|
    11|### createApp
    12|
    13|Create a new app inside the workspace. Writes template files into a subfolder and
    14|creates/updates the root rork.json manifest.
    15|
    16|When to call:
    17|- Empty workspace: you MUST call this before writing any code.
    18|- Existing workspace: call when the user asks to add another app (e.g. an iOS app alongside an
    19|  existing web app).
    20|
    21|Picking `framework` for the FIRST app:
    22|- Default to the user's selected platform (named in the new-project nudge).
    23|- Override only on clear intent for another platform — by name OR by use:
    24|  iPhone/iPad/App Store/widget → `swift`; Android/Play Store → `kotlin`;
    25|  website/dashboard/"deploy to my domain"/runs in a browser → `web`. Intent need not be the
    26|  exact word ("deploy it to my domain" → `web`); the app's genre alone is not intent (a
    27|  "CS2-style FPS is a PC game" stays on the selected platform).
    28|- createApp rejects the first attempt whose `framework` differs from the selected platform.
    29|  If the user really did ask for the other platform, just call createApp again with the same
    30|  `framework` and it goes through; otherwise switch to the selected platform.
    31|- `react-native` is only valid when the project was originally started with React Native;
    32|  the call is rejected otherwise. React native is deprecated and will be removed in the future.
    33|- Swift 3D games / RealityKit games → `swift`. Decide orientation from the requested genre,
    34|  camera, and controls before creating the app.
    35|- Use `swiftTemplateProfile: "landscape-3d-game"` when the chosen orientation is landscape:
    36|  explicit landscape/horizontal requests, generic or ambiguous Swift/RealityKit 3D games,
    37|  racing/driving/flight, FPS/TPS/shooters, arena combat, action RPG/open-world/adventure,
    38|  sports/fighting, dual-stick controls, wide tactical maps, or designs that need a wide
    39|  camera view or two-thumb controls.
    40|- Do not use the landscape profile for portrait-first games: endless/lane runners, vertical
    41|  runners/climbers/fallers, one-tap or swipe hypercasual games, idle/merge/tycoon/collection
    42|  games, card/board/puzzle/gacha games, vertical shooters, rhythm lane games, regular apps,
    43|  AR/model viewers, or non-game 3D showcases.
    44|
    45|Ask the user for a clear app name/alias, then call this tool with the name and framework.
    46|If a Swift/RealityKit 3D game's orientation is ambiguous, default to landscape and include
    47|`swiftTemplateProfile`; do not stop to ask the user. Omit the profile when the user
    48|explicitly requests portrait/vertical or the prompt clearly matches a portrait-first genre.
    49|The repo folder path is derived automatically from that name, for example `ios-client-demo`,
    50|`android-field-team`, or `web-marketing-site`.
    51|
    52|Temporary rollout constraint: only one app per framework can be created right now. If the
    53|project already has an iOS, Android, or Web app, do not create another one of that same
    54|framework yet.
    55|
    56|```ts
    57|type Input = {
    58|    /** Display name for the app (e.g. 'My Fitness Tracker') */
    59|    name: string;
    60|    /** Framework to use */
    61|    framework: "react-native" | "swift" | "kotlin" | "web";
    62|    /** Exact app folder path to create. If omitted, the path is derived from the app name. */
    63|    path?: string | undefined;
    64|    /** Swift-only template profile. Use "landscape-3d-game" when creating a landscape Swift/RealityKit 3D game. */
    65|    swiftTemplateProfile?: ("landscape-3d-game") | undefined;
    66|};
    67|```

(End of file - total 67 lines)

2. An abbreviation for something
3. Could be a social media app, a game, a utility...
